div.h3: Step-by-step File Descriptor (fd) Table & Kernel Object Manipulation

div.p: c int fd = open("output.txt", O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0644); dup2(fd, STDOUT_FILENO); // STDOUT_FILENO is 1 close(fd); // Clean up the temp fd
Key Fact: dup2 is atomic. It ensures that the redirection happens without a gap where the process might have no stdout.

ul.li: 0, 1, 2 all point to the same TTY device.
Writes to STDOUT (fd 1) appear on screen.
Allocates struct file in kernel memory.

ol.li: Copies fd 3 pointer into fd 1 slot.
Refcount for out.txt increments to 2. Effect: Any printf now goes to out.txt.

ul.li: File remains open because fd 1 still references it.

li.strong: Redirection is now clean and transparent.
